Charlton Street Gang.description: The Charlton Street Gang was a group of river pirates active on the Hudson River in New York City during the mid-19th century. They raided small cargo ships and later moved upriver, stealing a sloop in 1869 under leader Sadie the Goat. The gang engaged in kidnapping for ransom and flew the Jolly Roger flag. They dissolved by the end of the 1860s after conflicts with vigilantes.

Spook Hunters.description: White racist youth gang based in South Gate, California, active from the 1940s to the 1950s. Formed in reaction to the growing African American population in Los Angeles, the gang opposed integration and racial segregation, starting fights and intimidating black youths. Their activities prompted the formation of black protective gangs such as the Slausons.

descriptions: Spook Hunters attacked black youths, leading to formation of The Gladiators for self-defense.
edges.evidence_quote: In response to these white racist gangs, the black youths formed their own gangs to protect themselves and their neighborhoods: The Slausons, the Businessmen, and the Gladiators were all formed as a direct reaction to attacks from white gangs like the Spook Hunters.

It is involved in criminal activities such as extortion and fraud, including illicitly obtaining European Union funds. The clan has been targeted by law enforcement operations like Itaca-Freeboat and Pietranera, leading to arrests and convictions. As of 2017, its alleged boss Vincenzo Gallelli was imprisoned.

Arifs.description: The Arifs are a Turkish Cypriot criminal organization based in south-east London, involved in armed robbery, drug trafficking, and racketeering since the late 1960s. They rose to prominence after the Kray brothers' downfall and were considered Britain's No. 1 crime family in the 2000s. The gang is led by brothers Deniz, Mehmet, and Doğan Arif, with several members convicted in the 1990s. They maintain ties with Turkey for drug shipments.

Bonanno crime family.description: The Bonanno crime family is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the Five Families that dominate organized crime in New York City. Founded by Salvatore Maranzano in the 1890s, it was later led by Joseph Bonanno. The family has been involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, and labor union corruption, with operations extending into New Jersey, Florida, California, Arizona, and Montreal. Historically one of the smallest Five Families, it was known for its brutality and internal conflicts such as the Banana War.

Cartel del Norte del Valle.description: Colombian drug trafficking organization based in northern Valle del Cauca, active from the 1980s to 2008. It emerged after the fragmentation of the Cali Cartel in the mid-1990s and was known for its violence and internal betrayals. The cartel was led by Orlando Henao Montoya until his death in 1998, after which it split into factions led by Diego Montoya Sánchez and Wilber Alirio Varela. It was eventually dismantled, though small dissident groups remain active.

Mongols MC.description: The Mongols Motorcycle Club is an international outlaw motorcycle gang founded on December 5, 1969 in Montebello, California. The club is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, and violent crimes. It has chapters in 28 countries and an estimated membership of 4,000–5,000. The club is designated as an outlaw motorcycle gang by law enforcement in multiple countries.

Chosen Few Motorcycle Club.description: The Chosen Few Motorcycle Club is the first mixed-race 1%er outlaw motorcycle club, founded around 1959 in Los Angeles, California by Lionel Ricks and others.
